info.title: Zebra IoT Connector for Handheld Readers
info.description: Zebra IoT Connector API Documentation for Handheld Readers (RFD40/90) supported via Wi-Fi and Ethernet Interfaces. The interface is built on a web-friendly protocol MQTT and JSON-formatted data structures that can be leveraged by applications running on the cloud or on-premise to manage and control readers.

tags.name: Control
tags.description: Control commands are structured APIs that enable users to control Handheld RFID device operations. These commands facilitate actions such as starting or stopping inventory scans, retrieving or setting operating modes, and configuring post-filters, ensuring precise and efficient control over the device's functionality.

tags.name: Device Management
tags.description: The Device Management Commands are focused on managing IoT devices by performing tasks like configuration, endpoint management, and operational control. These commands enable efficient device administration, ensuring proper functionality and interaction with the system. They are key to streamlining device operations and maintaining effective management workflows.

tags.name: Events
tags.description: The events are triggered by specific occurrences in IoT systems, such as battery status changes, firmware updates, or network events. These events are used for monitoring system health, notifying users, handling exceptions, and logging system activities. They ensure efficient communication, timely interventions, and reliable operation of IoT ecosystems. Their purpose is to centralize event handling and streamline responses to various IoT scenarios.

post.description: This command is designed to fetch the current operating mode information of RFID device within the IOTC system

Get operating mode.description: Payload to get operating mode configurations

post.description: This operation is used to configure the operating mode of RFID devices. Note: When RFD40/90 sled is configured to FAST_READ profile, data events are not currently supported. On reboot the set configurations will be lost and the device will go back to default operating mode.

Set operating mode.description: Payload to set operating mode configurations

Profile setting.description: Payload to set operating mode profile type

Access operations.description: Payload to set the required access operations

Radio start/stop conditions.description: Payload to set inventory start and stop conditions

Select settings.description: Payload to set select pre-filters parameters

Query and select settings.description: Payload to set query and select parameters

Tag metadata settings.description: Payload to enable/disable tag metadata to be reported

post.description: This operation is used to set a post-filter for RFID device, enabling the filtering of tags scanned by the device based on specific criteria.

Set post filter 1.description: Payload to filter tags using prefix

Set post filter 2.description: Payload to filter tags based on regex

post.description: This command controls device functionality, including RFID operations and scan operations.
This operation is used to fetch the post filter applied to filter tag data in RFID devices. The post filter ensures that only relevant tag data is processed based on specified criteria.

Get post filter.description: Payload to get post filter configurations

post.description: Reboot command is used to perform warm reset of device. After successful reboot, device will reinitiate the connection to previously connected server. Upon failure notification will be sent.
This command is used to retrieve wifi configuration details

WPA2 Personal connection.description: Response with WiFi connected to WPA2 Personal network

WPA2 Enterprise connection.description: Response with WiFi connected to WPA2 Enterprise network with certificates

WPA3 Enterprise connection.description: Response with WiFi connected to WPA3 Enterprise network with advanced security

Open network connection.description: Response with WiFi connected to open/unsecured network

WiFi interface disabled.description: Response when WiFi interface is disabled on the device

post.description: This command used to retrieve the reader status information.

Device status info.description: Response with current device status including battery and connection details

post.description: Command issued to set new firmware & initiate firmware update

No authentication HTTP.description: Payload to initiate firmware update with no authentication

Pre-installed CA certificate HTTPS.description: Payload to initiate firmware update with pre-installed CA certificate content

Inline CA certificate HTTPS.description: Payload to initiate firmware update with inline CA certificate content

post.description: This API is used to set wifi configuration. It currently supports IPv4 addressing with DHCP-based IP assignment only; static IP configuration and IPv6 support is not available in the current API version.

Create WPA2 personal.description: Payload to create a new WiFi profile with WPA2 Personal security

Modify WPA2 personal.description: Payload to modify an existing WiFi profile password

Set preferred AP.description: Payload to set an access point as preferred

Create WPA2 enterprise.description: Payload to create a WiFi profile with WPA2 Enterprise security and certificates

post.description: Command issued to retrieve the complete device configuration such as version details, device status, current region details, ethernet & wifi configuration, end point configuration, details of installed certificates, event and alert configuration

Complete device config.description: Response with complete device configuration including all settings

post.description: Command issued to delete saved wifi profile

Delete wifi profile.description: Payload to delete a saved WiFi profile

response.description: WIFI Error - Cannot delete active SSID

post.description: This API used to retrieve ethernet configuration details. Security features, static IP configuration, and IPv6 support are not available in the current API version.

Ethernet config info.description: Response with ethernet interface configuration details

Ethernet interface disabled.description: Response when ethernet interface is disabled on the device

post.description: Enables or disables specific device events. To apply the changes, a device reboot is required. For RFD4090 devices, only the following events are supported: terminalConnection, firmwareUpdate, network, exceptions, ntp, heartbeat, power, battery, and fileDownload.

Enable all events.description: Payload to enable all event types with threshold configuration

Selective events.description: Payload to selectively enable/disable specific event types

post.description: This command is used to retrieve the reader information like device serial no, model no, sku, and firmware version information.

Device version info.description: Response with device serial, model, SKU and firmware version details

post.description: This command is used to retrieve the current region configuration of the device.

Current region info.description: Response with current device region configuration

post.description: Command issued to set the complete device configuration such as current region details, ethernet & wifi configuration, end point configuration,  event and alert configuration

Create wifi profile.description: Payload to create a new WiFi profile with WPA2 Personal security

Modify wifi profile.description: Payload to modify an existing WiFi profile and set as preferred

Add mdm endpoint.description: Payload to add a new MDM endpoint configuration with reboot setting

Add data endpoint.description: Payload to add a data endpoint configuration with MQTT parameters

Add control endpoint.description: Payload to add a control endpoint with publish/subscribe topics

Complete config setup.description: Payload to set complete configuration with WiFi and management endpoint

post.description: Command to get configured endpoints.If API does not included endpointName argument, then  response includes all active endpoints detailed configuration & list of all saved endpoint names otherwise response includes interested endpoints configuration only

Active endpoint config.description: Payload to get active endpoint configuration details with all saved endpoint names

Specific endpoint config.description: Payload to get the specified endpoint configurations

Active endpoint config.description: Payload to get active endpoint configuration details with all saved endpoint names

Specific endpoint config.description: Payload to get the specified endpoint configurations

post.description: This API is used to get installed certificates.
Using config_endpoint user can add/delete/modify the end point configuration

Add MGMT endpoint for MQTT TLS.description: Payload to add a new management endpoint configuration with MQTT TLS protocol

Add MDM endpoint.description: Payload to add a new MDM endpoint configuration

Delete endpoint.description: Payload to delete an existing endpoint configuration

Add data endpoint.description: Payload to add a new data endpoint configuration

Add control endpoint.description: Payload to add a new control endpoint with publish/subscribe topics

post.description: This API is used to install certificates.

MQTT certificates with certificate authe.description: Payload to install MQTT certificates using CA certificate

Filestore certificate with certificate a.description: Payload to install filestore CA certificate

MQTT certificates without authentication.description: Payload to install MQTT client certificates without authentication via HTTP

WiFi certificates without authentication.description: Payload to install WiFi certificates without authentication via HTTP

WiFi CA certificate using direct content.description: Installing WiFi CA certificate, where certificate content is provided as part of API

post.description: Command issued to delete saved certificate

Delete wifi ca cert.description: Payload to delete a WiFi CA certificate from the device

Delete mqtt client cert.description: Payload to delete an MQTT client certificate from the device

Delete filestore ca cert.description: Payload to delete a filestore CA certificate from the device

post.description: This event captures details about exception events in the IOTC system, including metadata and component-specific errors. Note: this is currently not supported.

scanner.message: scanner is connected and scanning in progress

post.description: Data Events capture and represent the structured information related to various events generated by the system. These events may include tag data, barcode data, and other relevant event-related details. They are utilized for tracking, monitoring, and analyzing system operations and behaviors in real-time or during post-event reviews.
Represents a system-generated alert or notification event. Note: Currently event types such as antenna, temperature, exceptions, CPU usage, GPI, and user app info are not supported.
This event represents a heartbeat signal sent periodically from a device or system to indicate its active status and provide essential metadata like uptime and event details.
Short form of alerts, does not contain details about the alert. This is mainly meant for SOTI.

Battery critical set.description: Alert indicating battery level is critically low

MQTT CA certificate download success.description: Notification indicating successful MQTT CA certificate download

MQTT CA certificate install success.description: Notification indicating successful MQTT CA certificate installation

WiFi client certificate download success.description: Notification indicating successful WiFi client certificate download

WiFi client certificate install success.description: Notification indicating successful WiFi client certificate installation

Filestore client key download fail.description: Notification indicating failure in filestore client key download

post.description: This event provides details about the connection state of a device, including its metadata and protocol versions.
